title: Baking Powder and Baking Soda
content:
BAKING POWDERS

Tartrate: Quick reaction; use immediately after mixing. Poor keeping qualities.

Phosphate: Slower reaction; releases most gas in cold dough.

Double-Acting (S.A.S.): Starts in cold dough, main rising action in oven heat. Recommended for most baking.

BAKING SODA

Requires acid ingredient (sour milk, molasses, chocolate) to produce leavening. Substitute: ½ teaspoon soda + ½ cup sour milk = 1 teaspoon baking powder. Do not reduce below ½ teaspoon per cup sour milk even at high altitude.

Substitution Formulas:
- ½ teaspoon cream of tartar + ¼ teaspoon soda + ¼ teaspoon salt = 1 teaspoon baking powder
- High altitude: decrease baking powder ⅛-¼ teaspoon per teaspoon
